title: diploglottis cunninghamii var. muelleri
intro: Diploglottis cunninghamii var. muelleri, also known as the Queensland or Australian finger lime, is a variety of the Diploglottis cunninghamii species, a type of citrus fruit native to the tropical regions of Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of the Queensland finger lime include its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and limonoids, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. The plant's herbal preparations include using the fruit pulp, seeds, and leaves in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
